title: Anomaly-Driven Correction Discovery (ADCD): Physics-Constrained Symbolic Regression for Evolutionary Scientific Discovery
description:
Anomaly-Driven Correction Discovery (ADCD) is a physics-constrained symbolic regression framework that discovers physical correction terms rather than learning equations from scratch — mimicking the evolutionary nature of scientific discovery.

Instead of a blank-slate symbolic regression search, ADCD takes a known classical law as a baseline and searches for dimensionless correction factors Δ that explain the residual anomaly between classical theory and experimental observation. This mirrors how science actually evolves: Newton → Einstein (GR), Dirac → QED, Rayleigh-Jeans → Planck.

Pipeline (3 stages)

  • Stage 1 — Cascaded Physics Gates: AST complexity gate, dimensional homogeneity + transcendental guardrails, Asymptotic Regression Consistency (ARC), coarse numerical evaluator.
  • Stage 2 — Parameter Optimization: JAX-traced L-BFGS-B with parameter scaling, multi-restart log-uniform (15 restarts), auto loss-mode switching.
  • Stage 3 — Occam’s Razor: BIC reranking + AST node-count tie-breaker, structural classification.

Headline results

  • 9-scenario benchmark (seed=42): 94.4% structural recovery
  • 5-seed multi-seed (Tier B+): 82.8% ± 7.7%
  • vs PySR fair @ 5% noise: 88.9% vs 11.1% (77.8 pp gap)
  • Real-world: 4/4 structural (Mercury perihelion, Lamb Shift, Muon g-2, Blackbody radiation)

v2.2.0 — Phase 2 (Multivariable Discovery)

  • Buckingham Π group engine (buckingham_pi.py)
  • Sequential ARC (sequential_arc.py): per-variable asymptotic-limit gate
  • Residual Factorizer v2 (residual_factorizer_v2.py): variance-decomposition separability detection
  • Multivariable Orchestrator (multivar_orchestrator.py): end-to-end multi-input correction search
  • Phase 2 benchmark: 2/4 multivariable scenarios solved

See the repository for full source, tests, the 23-page paper (PDF included as supplementary), and benchmark data.
